title: crupina vulgaris subsp. brachypappa
intro: Crupina vulgaris subsp. brachypappa, also known as the short-stemmed crupina, is a flowering plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, among others. Herbal preparations of Crupina vulgaris subsp. brachypappa can be made into teas, infusions, and topical creams, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
